prompt: A realistic indoor photograph of a wooden chessboard on a dark wooden table, viewed from a slightly elevated front angle, with Staunton-style pieces in a mid-opening position. The board has light maple and dark walnut squares with a wooden border, and the pieces are glossy black and matte ivory. Show all 32 pieces with no captures: 16 black pieces and 16 white pieces. Black occupies the far side in standard back-rank order from left to right: rook, knight, bishop, queen, king, bishop, knight, rook, with 8 black pawns in front. White occupies the near side in standard back-rank order from left to right: rook, knight, bishop, queen, king, bishop, knight, rook, with 8 white pawns in front. The only moved pieces are 2 central pawns: a black pawn advanced two squares to e5 and a white pawn advanced two squares to e4, facing each other on adjacent central squares. Keep the rest of the pieces in their starting positions. Use natural window light from the right, soft shadows, a warm home interior background with cream curtains and a wooden cabinet, shallow depth of field, realistic proportions, clean digital-photography look, centered composition, and accurate chessboard geometry.
